step_by_step_thinking:
I agree with the summary provided by agent2. Radiation-induced thyroid cancer is indeed classified as a somatic effect because it involves direct DNA damage to thyroid gland cells without altering the germline genome. This ensures that while these cancers may develop after exposure, there will be no inheritance pattern associated with them since the affected tissues are somatically derived and not part of reproductive cells.

Therefore, my final answer remains: A. Somatic.
raw_response:

Radiation-induced thyroid cancer is indeed classified as a somatic effect because it involves direct DNA damage to thyroid gland cells without altering the germline genome. This ensures that while these cancers may develop after exposure, there will be no inheritance pattern associated with them since the affected tissues are somatically derived and not part of reproductive cells.

Therefore, my final answer remains: A. Somatic.
